text:
REFINED BRIEFING (events grouped by probative direction).
Retrieval hints: actors=['Justin Trudeau', 'Prime Minister of Canada', 'Canadian Cabinet', 'U.S. President', 'U.S. Cabinet-level official'], topics=['canada-us relations', 'diplomacy', 'travel', 'bilateral meetings'].

## BACKGROUND (12)
- [2025-01-18] President-elect Donald Trump has extended invitations to foreign leaders for his upcoming inauguration. Chinese President Xi Jinping will be represented by Vice President Han Zheng, an announcement made on January 17, 2025. Trump and Xi held a phone call on January 17, discussing trade, fentanyl, and TikTok. Argentine President Javier Milei and Paraguayan President Santiago Peña attended the Hispanic Inaugural Ball on January 18, 2025. Milei is also scheduled to attend an official inaugural ball and the swearing-in ceremony. Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni is confirmed to attend the swearing-in. Other attendees include former Georgian President Salome Zourabichvili, Eric Zemmour, Marion Maréchal, and Ecuadorean President Daniel Noboa. Taiwanese delegates, initially invited, will not attend the outdoor ceremony due to weather but plan meetings with U.S. politicians.
- [2025-01-23] U.S. President Donald Trump is scheduled to hold a phone call with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele on Thursday, January 23, 2025, at 3:30 p.m. Washington time. The White House schedule indicates this is the first officially announced call with a foreign leader since Trump’s inauguration earlier this week. The call is set to occur after Trump's virtual participation in the Davos Forum, a private intelligence briefing, and the signing of new executive orders. Trump had an initial outreach to Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman on Wednesday. Bukele advocates cryptocurrency and tough-on-crime policies and was invited to Trump's inauguration.
- [2025-01-20] Massad Fares Boulos assumed the role of U.S. Senior Advisor for Arab and African Affairs on January 20, 2025, in President Donald Trump's second administration. He also assumed the position of Senior Advisor for Africa at the U.S. Department of State on April 1, 2025. President-elect Donald Trump designated Boulos as his Middle East adviser in December 2024. In these roles, Boulos serves as a Senior Advisor to the President on Arab and Middle Eastern Affairs and concurrently as Senior Advisor for Africa at the Department of State. Boulos is the father of Michael Boulos, who is married to Trump's daughter Tiffany. He campaigned for Trump's 2024 presidential campaign, engaging with Arab American communities.
- [2025-01-20] The U.S. Senate confirmed Marco Rubio as Secretary of State on Monday, making him the first member of President Donald Trump's new Cabinet. The vote was unanimous, 99-0, on Inauguration Day. Rubio, a Republican senator from Florida, had his nomination advanced by the Senate Foreign Relations Committee earlier on Monday. As Secretary of State, Rubio would serve as the nation's top diplomat and would be the first Latino to hold the position. He has a background in foreign affairs, particularly concerning South America and China.
- [2025-01-22] President Donald J. Trump held his first foreign leader call with Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia on Wednesday. The leaders discussed efforts to enhance Middle East stability, bolster regional security, and combat terrorism. They also discussed Saudi Arabia’s international economic ambitions for the next four years, and trade opportunities aimed at increasing mutual prosperity between the two countries.
